meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: Overcome Testing Gaps
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and bypass the need for deep testing knowledge. Learn how to ship production-ready apps using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Stop letting testing anxiety stall your deployments. By leveraging Antigravity to scaffold robust architecture and Claude Code to automate unit test generation, you can implement dynamic SaaS pricing models with absolute confidence. This workflow turns complex testing requirements into a hands-off, automated process, allowing you to ship faster and earn more.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, the client deadline is looming, and your feature-rich SaaS pricing model is stuck in a purgatory of 'maybe it works.' For the solo freelancer, the wall between shipping a high-ticket SaaS and drowning in bugs is usually a lack of testing infrastructure. You don't need a degree in QA; you need a system that treats testing as a byproduct of development, not a roadblock.

We are moving beyond the era of manual checklists. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent, context-aware testing, we turn the daunting 'testing phase' into a streamlined, automated pipeline. This is how the top 1% of AI builders ship reliable, high-value code while everyone else is still searching for syntax errors.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Logic Schema
steps.description: Before coding, establish the source of truth for your SaaS pricing. We use Antigravity to generate a type-safe schema that prevents runtime errors before they occur.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a type-safe TypeScript schema for a SaaS subscription engine handling tiered pricing (Free, Pro, Enterprise) including stripe-compatible metadata fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --context ./schema.ts --prompt 'Generate unit tests for all pricing edge cases'

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Controller
steps.description: Build the core logic that calculates the billing cycle. This step ensures your math is sound without manual verification.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust pricing controller function that calculates pro-rated subscription costs based on subscription start date and tier upgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file ./controllers/pricing.ts --instruction 'Fix any logic gaps found during execution'

steps.title: Automating Test Coverage
steps.description: Use Claude Code to read your logic and generate comprehensive test suites, effectively bypassing the need for manual testing knowledge.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the pricing controller and generate a complete Vitest suite covering happy paths, edge cases, and invalid input scenarios.

steps.title: Integrating Payment Hooks
steps.description: Secure your pricing implementation by simulating webhook events, ensuring your logic handles real-world payment failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a mock Stripe webhook handler that validates subscription status updates against our pricing schema.

steps.title: Final Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Perform a final automated audit of the entire pricing module to ensure zero regression before client handoff.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a full system audit of the pricing module and flag any potential vulnerabilities or logical errors in the billing flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Ship production-ready pricing module with 100% test coverage'

pro_tips: Treat your pricing schema as the single source of truth; if it isn't in the schema, it shouldn't be in the database.
Use Claude Code to generate 'negative test cases' specifically—ask it to try and break your pricing math.
Never manually write a test until you have asked the AI to scaffold the boilerplate; focus your energy on the edge cases.
Map your pricing tiers to environment variables so you can update them without deploying new code.
Always run a 'smoke test' script that simulates a full user signup and upgrade flow before every production push.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, move on to implementing automated user onboarding flows using the same Antigravity-to-Claude-Code pipeline to maintain consistency.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with testing and start shipping software that clients actually pay for. Our community provides the templates, the prompts, and the accountability you need to scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
